chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No. D-4992 of 2024 Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Muhammad Osman Ali Hadi [ United Friends Contractors Association V. Province of Sindh & others ] Date of hearing: 08.04.2025 Date of decision: 08.04.2025 Petitioner: Through Mr. Altamash Arab, Advocate Respondent No.2: Through M/s. Ahmed Ali Hussain & Hassan Ali Afandi, Advocates. Interveners: Through Mr. Amir Mansoob Qureshi, Advocate. Official Respondent(s): Through Mr. Muhammad Hisham Mahar, Assistant, Attorney General, Sindh. O R D E R Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J: Petitioner who is a contractor has impugned the work orders issued for completion of at least 85 schemes, the schedule of which, he has filed at Page Nos.61 onwards, 69 onwards & 76 onwards of the File. In the Petition, he has filed copies of those work orders purportedly issued in favour of Respondent Nos. 4 to 9, made party through amended title, which is taken on record today. 2. KMC, the procuring agency has filed comments, stating that no work order has been issued in respect of those schemes and the work orders filed with the Petition are fake and forged documents. Even, the private parties Respondent Nos.4 to 9 in whose favour the work orders have been issued purportedly confirm that no work order has been issued in their favour and the subject work orders are manipulated and maneuvered. Learned counsel for Respondent has categorically stated that all the schemes would be approved in accordance with law and funds will be released after observing all formalities under SPPRA Rules. The tender process would be made in accordance with law and SPPRA Rules and work order be assigned purely on the merit.

Learned counsel for Respondent has categorically stated that all the schemes would be approved in accordance with law and funds will be released after observing all formalities under SPPRA Rules. The tender process would be made in accordance with law and SPPRA Rules and work order be assigned purely on the merit. Learned counsel for the Petitioner is satisfied and this Petition in view of the statement made by learned counsel for Respondent – KMC is accordingly disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE M. Khan
